demographics.living_situation: lives at home with a live-in caregiver

opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: I'm so dizzy I can't stand up, my legs are all swollen, and I took all the pills from both boxes because I didn't know which ones were the right ones.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: She's been going downhill over the last two days — dizzy, sick in the stomach and her ankles have puffed up — and we're not sure what tablets she's actually meant to be taking after the specialist changed things.

symptom_narrative.onset: over the last 48 hours, getting worse
symptom_narrative.character: dizzy and unsteady — especially when she tries to stand — feeling sick, and her legs have swelled up; she's a bit muddled about her tablets
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: bad enough that she can't get out of bed and is frustrated and frightened
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: dizziness in her head, swelling in both lower legs
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: constant over two days and worse on standing
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: lying down seems to help the dizziness a little
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: standing up or trying to walk
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: nausea; feeling weak and confused about which medicines to take
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: normally walks independently with a frame, but is bedbound today because she gets too dizzy to stand

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: heart failure — takes fluid tablets for it

current_medications_as_reported.name_as_patient_states: fluid (water) tablets — she thinks there are two different boxes now
diabetes tablets — a couple of different ones

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: frail older woman living at home with a live-in caregiver who is feeling overwhelmed; the specialist recently changed her medicines and she got confused and took extra doses from more than one box

objective_data_offered.value: the home blood-pressure machine showed a low reading and her pulse felt like it was racing when she sat up
both lower legs look puffy and swollen; she looks pale and drowsy and is slumped back in the bed
she is too dizzy to stand, keeps feeling sick, and is muddled about which tablets she has already taken today
dozens of loose medication blister packs scattered on the kitchen table, including two different water-tablet boxes, and loose pills spilled around
